title: Orang National Park
tagline: Orang National Park — wildlife reserve of Assam

overview.short: Orang National Park is a national park in India located on the northern bank of the Brahmaputra River in the Darrang and Sonitpur districts of Assam.
overview.description: Orang National Park is a national park in India located on the northern bank of the Brahmaputra River in the Darrang and Sonitpur districts of Assam. It covers an area of 79.28 km2 (30.61 mi2). It was established as a sanctuary in 1985 and declared a national park on 13 April 1999. It is rich in flora and fauna, including great Indian rhinoceros, pygmy hog, Asian elephant, wild water buffalo and the Bengal tiger.

topPlaces.name: Brahmaputra Valley
topPlaces.description: The Brahmaputra Valley (also Assam Valley) is a region situated between hill ranges of the eastern Himalayan range of Assam in Northeastern India. The valley consists of the Western Brahmaputra Valley covering the regions of Goalpara and Kamrup; Central Brahmaputra Valley region covering Darrang, Nagaon and the North Bank and Eastern Brahmaputra Valley comprising districts of Sonitpur, Lakhimpur, Dibrugarh and Sivasagar.

items.note: The Brahmaputra Valley (also Assam Valley) is a region situated between hill ranges of the eastern Himalayan range of Assam in Northeastern India. The valley consists of the Western Brahmaputra Valley covering the regions of Goalpara and Kamrup; Central Brahmaputra Valley region covering Darrang, Nagaon and the North Bank and Eastern Brahmaputra Valley comprising districts of Sonitpur, Lakhimpur, Dibrugarh and Sivasagar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Orang National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Orang National Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Orang National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, Guwahati (95 km) and the nearest railway station is Guwahati Railway Station (77 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Orang National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Orang National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Orang National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Orang National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Orang National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Brahmaputra Valley.
